error_message: OTGW-firmware.ino:147:9: error: 'sscanf_P' was not declared in this scope; did you mean 'sscanf'? (env:esp32-combo only)
root_cause: sscanf_P is an ESP8266/AVR pgmspace helper; it does not exist in the ESP32 Arduino core. The new appendBootDetectLog() helper (TASK-848) is compiled only under HAS_RUNTIME_HW_DETECT, which is the combo (ESP32-S3) board. The esp8266 and plain esp32 envs skip the helper entirely (#if'd out), so build.py's default esp8266+esp32 pass was green and masked it; only env:esp32-combo actually compiles the code.
fix: Use plain sscanf() instead of sscanf_P(). PSTR() is a no-op on ESP32 so the format-string-in-flash convention is preserved (sscanf(first, PSTR("boot#%u"), &prev)). Also a process note: combo-only code must be built with `pio run -e esp32-combo` (via ~/.platformio/penv pio) — build.py only targets esp8266+esp32, so HAS_RUNTIME_HW_DETECT paths are NOT covered by the default build.

error_message: build.bat 'broke' — esp32 produces no assets while esp8266 builds fine. Symptoms: 'Assembler messages: Fatal error: can't create .pio/build/esp32/.../FCString.cpp.o: No such file or directory', 'fatal error: opening dependency file .../X.cpp.d: No such file or directory', missing library headers (wm_strings_en.h, copyReplace.h, KString.h, NetApiHelpers.h), and 'FileNotFoundError: ...\.pio\build\esp32\.sconsign311.tmp'. On a later run: 'tool-esptoolpy ... PackageException: Could not install package' and tool-esptoolpy dropped out of the PACKAGES list.
file: (environment, not source) ~/.platformio + .pio shared between two concurrent builds
root_cause: NOT a source commit. build.py at HEAD (== fb641d26, ADR-124 fully reverted) is intact. A SECOND build was running concurrently in the same worktree: PID 39868 'C:\...\Python314\python.exe build.py' (full build under system Python 3.14, bypassing build.bat which pins .build-python 3.12). Two 'pio run -e esp32' processes shared one ~/.platformio package cache AND one .pio/build/esp32 + .pio/libdeps/esp32 tree. They shredded each other's intermediate .o/.d files, headers, scons sign db, and raced on tool-esptoolpy (re)install -> the package cache itself corrupted (esp32s3.py/esp8266.py target files vanished). Exactly the 'two sessions, one worktree' collision documented in CLAUDE.md/memory. The masked 'exit 0' from the first `cmd /c build.bat > log` run was a red herring: Git Bash `cmd /c build.bat` does not redirect build.py output (log captured only the cmd banner), so that exit code was meaningless.
fix: Kill the rogue concurrent build (taskkill /PID 39868 /T /F killed the whole pio/scons tree), then `rm -rf .pio/build/esp32` and rebuild SOLO. A single `.build-python\python.exe build.py` (no flags) then completed green: 4 pio envs succeeded, both targets produced firmware + littlefs + merged + flash.zip. Prevention: never run two builds in the same worktree; one worktree per concurrent session, or background sub-agents serialised through one main thread (per CLAUDE.md). Diagnosis tool: `Get-CimInstance Win32_Process` filtered on python.exe/pio.exe/scons to spot the second build.

error_message: SAT view: when manual settings are selected the panel does not resize to fit the revealed controls (overflow/clipping). Field report @sergeantd, OTGW32, alpha.99, still present alpha.139 (TASK-812).
root_cause: SAT settings rows (.settingDiv built in index.js buildSatSettings) live inside .sat-settings-group-body, which is NOT a grid container. The main settings page wraps the same .settingDiv inside .settings-group-body and applies float-neutralisation + responsive flex/grid overrides (components.css:769-798). The SAT body has no equivalent override, so SAT rows fall back to the legacy float-based .settingDiv layout: a fixed 320px float:left label plus an uncapped input/select. The legacy width rule (.settingDiv input[...], line 711-713) lists only text/password/number and OMITS select, so a <select> with a long option (e.g. a manual sensor address like 'AA:BB:.. (manual)') grows to its widest option and pushes the row wider than a narrow group body -> horizontal overflow, no wrap. NOTE: the exact control @sergeantd hit is unconfirmed (no reveal-on-select logic exists; phrasing is his). This is a proven layout-asymmetry fix and the best match to the symptom class, not a screenshot-confirmed repro.
fix: Added .sat-settings-group-body > .settingDiv override block in components.css mirroring the main-settings float-neutralisation WITHOUT subgrid (parent is not a grid): float:none, width:auto, min-width:0, flex-wrap on the row; label wraps (overflow-wrap:anywhere); input AND select get min-width:0 / max-width:100% so the row sizes to the panel. Did not copy the main page's grid subgrid rules (would break on a non-grid parent).

fix: Added local Miniconda-based Python bootstrap fallback, downloader abstraction, pip ensurepip/get-pip fallback, and repo PYTHONPATH export for self-contained execution

error_message: ESP32 build: 'settingsDirty' was not declared in this scope
root_cause: Phase-1 research suggested setting settingsDirty=true directly to trigger the settings-flush debounce, but settingsDirty is a file-static (private to settingStuff.ino:21) and not externally accessible. Cross-file write would require exposing it as extern, breaking encapsulation.
fix: Route the dirty-trigger through updateSetting("SATblerostercount", "<n>"). updateSetting() is the public API that already sets settingsDirty=true and RESTART_TIMER(timerFlushSettings) at lines 979-980. Same effect, encapsulation preserved.

error_message: ESP32 -Wvolatile deprecation warning on chained volatile-qualified assignment
root_cause: TASK-506 wrote `a = b = c = d = e = 0;` where all targets are volatile uint32_t. C++20 (DR2138) deprecates this idiom: the value of an assignment to a volatile lvalue is itself volatile and chaining propagates that. ESP8266 toolchain accepts it; ESP32-S3 xtensa-esp gcc warns.
fix: Reset each counter on its own line. Verbose but C++20-clean and forward-compatible.

error_message: PreToolUse hook blocked Edit on data/index.js: innerHTML with untrusted content
root_cause: First version of buildBleSensorsPanel() / renderBleRoster() built panel HTML via .innerHTML with template-string-interpolated user data. Even with an HTML-escape helper for labels and strict regex MAC validation, the security hook blocks innerHTML on principle when interpolation touches user-controlled text.
fix: Refactored to pure DOM API: createElement + textContent + appendChild for every node. No innerHTML anywhere in the new BLE panel code. Sensor labels go through textContent which the browser cannot interpret as HTML.

root_cause: Had "How often to fetch weather data in seconds (300-3
fix: Changed to "How often to fetch weather data in seconds (900-3

error_message: TASK-530 field reports interpreted `hd_drip_cooldown_skip` on ESP32-S3 as heap-threshold throttling, but the counter was actually driven by the post-status-burst cooldown gate inherited from ESP8266 pacing.
root_cause: The 2.0.0 branch used one discovery-drip policy for both ESP8266 and ESP32. On ESP32, the 2000ms post-burst cooldown and ESP8266-oriented drip guard comments made normal discovery pacing look like heap pressure even when free heap and max alloc block were healthy.
fix: Made the discovery-drip policy platform-aware: ESP32 now uses a 250ms post-status-burst cooldown, a lower last-ditch publish floor, and an ESP32-specific slow-mode guard based on both free heap and largest allocatable block. Updated MQTT API docs to clarify that `drip_cooldown_skip` is a pacing counter and that `max_block` maps to `platformMaxFreeBlock()`.

error_message: Rapid WebUI reloads could leave stale OT-log WebSocket clients alive long enough to exhaust the three-client limit before the firmware reaped them.
root_cause: The 2.0.0 WebUI kept the OT-log WebSocket open when a tab was hidden and did not explicitly close it on page unload or reload. A burst of reloads could therefore stack old sockets faster than heartbeat cleanup reclaimed them, making the next page instance look like a firmware freeze.
fix: Added pagehide/beforeunload OT-log shutdown in index.js, persisted the OT-log buffer before unload, cancelled delayed reconnect timers, and delayed the next main-page reconnect by 250 ms. Added 5-second WebSocket burst diagnostics in webSocketStuff.ino so reload retests can distinguish churn from max-client, low-heap, or error bursts.

error_message: Windows build.bat failed on clean environment and later lost firmware artifacts during buildfs phase
root_cause: build.bat required preinstalled Python and embedded Python lacked repo import path; build.py esptool auto-install could fail without setuptools/wheel; and PlatformIO buildfs on Windows could clean .pio artifacts causing missing firmware.bin before merge step.
fix: Added portable Python bootstrap in build.bat (download + extract + pip bootstrap), repaired embedded python*._pth with repo path/import site, hardened esptool installation in build.py by preinstalling setuptools/wheel and better pip strategy, and switched to phased artifact collection (firmware first, filesystem second) to preserve binaries across build phases.

error_message: BLE sensor roster empty in WebUI; user cannot select a BLE sensor (sergeantd, alpha.84, OTGW32/ESP32-S3)
root_cause: ESP32 chunked-JSON byte-ordering. The TASK-529 coalescing TX buffer (sTxBuf, jsonStuff.ino) buffers sendJsonMapEntry/sendStartJsonMap/sendBeforenext via restSendContent and flushes at restFlushContent, but satBLERosterSendJSON wrote the sensors array with raw httpServer.sendContent(_P) which bypasses the buffer and hits the socket immediately. Raw array bytes emitted BEFORE buffered wrapper -> invalid JSON ("sensors":[..]{meta..}) -> r.json() rejects -> .catch swallowed -> empty roster. ESP8266 unaffected (no buffer). Same defect in sendSensorStatus (restAPI.ino) and SATweather forecast block.
fix: Route the raw array/sub-object writes through restSendContent/restSendContentP so they share the coalescing buffer and byte order is preserved. Also surfaced parse failure in index.js renderBleRoster. Shipped alpha.88 (e57e1cce).

error_message: Graph legend + panel-title text unreadable (dark grey) on dark theme; should be white
root_cause: init() derived currentTheme from localStorage("theme"), wrong key. Canonical key driving body.dark + design tokens is "otgw-theme". On fresh load with saved dark theme, currentTheme stayed light -> ECharts used light palette/fallback (#4a4d50) on dark surface. Regression of TASK-568 (which only set the color token, not the theme source).
fix: Derive currentTheme from document.body.classList.contains("dark"); switch legend+title textStyle from --fg-2 (#e0e0e0) to --fg-1 (white in dark), fallback aligned. Shipped alpha.87 (80b25a80).
Rewrote 17â†’23 lines (4 removed) | Also: // Auto-off after timeout; if (oledPage != 0 && (now - oledLastActivity) >= O

fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

error_message: Wrong reference: Proposed should be Accepted
git commit exit 1; .githooks/pre-commit exits 2 (silent in PowerShell)
root_cause: MISDIAGNOSED INITIALLY as a broken python3 WindowsApps alias. Verified false: python3 --version reports Python 3.14.2 and runs adr-judge fine (resolves to pythoncore-3.14-64). The SOLE cause was adr-judge 0.15.0 rejecting ADR-099's enforcement block: the 'guidance' key is not allowed by the v0.14.0+ schema, so the judge aborted (exit 2) before evaluating any diff. Secondary observation (not the failure cause): OTGW's .githooks/pre-commit was a stale copy that hardcoded python3 and lacked the version-validated detection adr-kit's template already ships (>= v0.14.1).
fix: Real fix: removed the 'guidance' key from ADR-099's enforcement block (mechanical schema migration; intent preserved in the ## Decision section, which the LLM judge reads). Also re-synced OTGW's pre-commit adr-kit section to mirror the canonical adr-kit template (version-validated Python detection + nullglob SemVer cache pick) to end hook drift. No adr-kit repo change needed: upstream already had robust detection.

error_message: cc1plus.exe: fatal error: src/OTGW-firmware/OTGW-firmware.ino.cpp: No such file or directory (esp32 firmware FAILED)
root_cause: Ran 'pio run -e esp8266' and 'pio run -e esp32' in parallel in the same worktree. The arduino sketch-concat step (.ino -> .ino.cpp) shares an intermediate across envs, so the two concurrent runs raced and the esp32 .ino.cpp was missing at compile time. Separate .pio/build/<env> dirs are NOT enough isolation; the concat step collides. Filesystem builds (buildfs, no concat) parallelize fine.
fix: rm -rf .pio/build/esp32 then rebuild solo: 'pio run -e esp32' -> SUCCESS (Flash 95.2%). For genuinely parallel firmware builds use separate git worktrees, not just separate pio envs.

error_message: Settings cards render full-width with inputs flung to far right (both themes); grid/subgrid compact layout broken on OTGW32 alpha.92
root_cause:
Regression from 1a01bb82. normalizeSettingsLabelWidth() measured all .settings-group-body .settings-field-container including the WiFi-scan panel heading + full-width

(which reuse that class), poisoning --settings-label-w. Also #wifi-scan-panel was a plain grid item whose wide max-content inflated the label column track, stretching every label.
fix: Scoped the measurement selector to .settings-group-body .settingDiv > .settings-field-container (real row labels only); added CSS .settings-group-body > #wifi-scan-panel { grid-column: 1 / -1 } so the panel spans both columns like .fixed-ip-section. Verified in a Playwright harness: labelVar 276px, cards 520px.

error_message: id-254 SAT flame_status HA discovery entity was never published
root_cause: mqttHaSensors[] held 386 rows but MQTT_HA_SENSOR_COUNT was 385. Discovery loop in doAutoConfigureMsgid (MQTTstuff.ino:2330) is `while (sIdx < MQTT_HA_SENSOR_COUNT)`; id 254 flame_status has first index 385, so 385<385 is false and the row was skipped. Latent off-by-one introduced when the id-254 row was added without bumping COUNT.
fix: TASK-754 removed the deprecated picavailable sensor row (ADR-113 stage 2), bringing the table to 385 rows == COUNT. id 254 first index is now 384 < 385, so flame_status (live publisher at SATcontrol.ino:2186) discovers correctly. Index offsets for ids 250-254 decremented by 1; evaluate.py HA Sensor Index Consistency PASS.

error_message: Wrong reference: SAT_WIN4H_SIZE should be buffer
Duplicate inline platformWiFiIsEncrypted() in platform_esp32.h committed+pushed (9fa1f6f4); ESP32 redefinition masked by build.py exit 0
root_cause: Two Edit calls inserted the same shim at the same anchor (first edit shifted the anchor, second re-inserted). build.py wrapper returns 0 even when a per-env PlatformIO compile fails, so the duplicate-definition error was not surfaced by the wrapper exit code.
fix: Removed the duplicate copy; rebuilt and verified per-env: esp8266 SUCCESS AND esp32 SUCCESS by grepping the build log, not trusting the wrapper exit. Lesson: always verify per-env SUCCESS lines in the build log.

error_message: MQTTHaDiscovery.cpp: 'OTlookup_t'/'OTmap'/'OT_MSGID_MAX'/'PROGMEM_readAnything' was not declared in this scope (esp8266 FAILED, build.py exited 0 masking it)
root_cause: MQTTHaDiscovery.cpp is a separate .cpp translation unit that only includes MQTTstuff.h + platform.h, NOT OTGW-Core.h, so OTmap and the OTlookup helpers (visible to .ino files in the single Arduino TU) are not declared there.
fix: Do not resolve the OTmap label inside the .cpp. Pass the label string as a const char* parameter to streamOverrideSensorDiscovery; the dispatcher in MQTTstuff.ino (a .ino, sees OTmap) resolves it via messageIDToString and passes it in.

error_message: Settings page: static-IP rows show only 2 of 4 octet inputs, octets stretched, field labels wrap to two lines
root_cause: .fixed-ip-section is a .settingDiv direct child of .settings-group-body, so the broad fill rule ".settings-group-body > .settingDiv input[type=text] { flex:1; min-width:80px }" (components.css:791) matches the nested .octet-input fields and overrides their 38px width. Four stretched octets overflow the 520px card and wrap; .fixed-ip-section overflow:hidden clips the second row.
fix: Added higher-specificity reset ".settings-group-body > .settingDiv .octet-group .octet-input { flex:0 0 auto; min-width:0; width:38px }" plus ".fixed-ip-fields .fixed-ip-row > .fixed-ip-field-label { flex:1 1 auto; min-width:0 }" so the four octets fit one compact box and the label takes the row slack.

error_message: Static-IP octet inputs clip 3-digit values (192 shows 19, 255 shows 25) after the overflow fix
root_cause: Generic .settingDiv input[type=text] { font-size:12pt } (specificity 0,2,1) outweighs .octet-input { font-size: var(--fs-sm) } (0,1,0). Octets rendered at 12pt and overran the 38px box, hiding the third digit.
fix: Added font-size:var(--fs-sm) to the higher-specificity octet reset (.settings-group-body > .settingDiv .octet-group .octet-input) and widened width 38px->42px.

error_message: SAT narration lines (prefix S) would mis-parse as garbled OT frames in the Web UI live-log; SAT-only filter predicate (d.source===S) never matched
root_cause: parseLogLine event-prefix regex only recognized > < ! * — not S. SAT lines fell through to the fixed-width OT-frame column parser, so source became the first 18 chars, not S, and the line rendered as a malformed frame.
fix: Extended the event regex to /^([><!*S]) (.*)/ so S lines parse as events {isEvent:true, prefix:S, label:msg}; filter predicate became isEvent && prefix===S. Caught because the original Playwright test only seeded strings (false confidence); rewrote it to seed production parsed objects.

error_message: OTGW32 SAT dashboard tiles all show '--' (NaN). Browser console: sat.js:156 [SAT] fetch error: SyntaxError: Unterminated fractional number in JSON at position 4096 / Expected ',' or '}' after property value. Field report @sergeantd, alpha.150, only after enabling+selecting a BLE sensor. Values fill briefly then disappear. (TASK-764)
root_cause: satBLESendStatusJSON() (called mid-stream by satSendStatusJSON) emitted ble_temp and ble_humidity via raw httpServer.sendContent(json) instead of restSendContent(json). On ESP32 (HAS_REST_TX_COALESCING=1) REST output is batched into a 4096-byte coalescing buffer (sTxBuf, jsonStuff.ino). A raw httpServer.sendContent flushes its chunk to the wire IMMEDIATELY, jumping ahead of the ~4KB of SAT fields still buffered in sTxBuf, so the JSON bytes hit the wire OUT OF ORDER -> scrambled JSON -> JSON.parse fails around the 4095-byte flush boundary (~position 4096). Only manifests on ESP32 (ESP8266 flushes inline, same order), only on the >4KB SAT status response, and only when state.sat.bBleTempValid is true (raw branch executes). Server logs 200/chunks=3 with valid data (room=19.5 outside=8.4) -- only wire order was wrong, not the data. The sibling satBLERosterSendJSON() was already fixed for this exact gotcha (its comment at SATble.ino:750-753 documents it); satBLESendStatusJSON was missed.
fix: Replaced the two raw httpServer.sendContent(json) calls at SATble.ino:696 and :700 with restSendContent(json) so ble_temp/ble_humidity route through the same coalescing buffer as every other field, preserving wire order.

error_message: OTGW32 web UI 'cannot find sat-slider.js' (and index.js, sat.js, css) - resources never load in the browser, alpha.160.
root_cause: NOT a 404 / not a stale LittleFS image. sat-slider.js fetched in isolation returns 200/1736 bytes/679ms - the file is present and serves fine. The browser fires ~8 local sub-resource requests in parallel after index.html; the OTGW32 single-threaded webserver starves and the sub-resource requests stall and never complete (no-response) while the page document (200) and the external echarts CDN (274ms) succeed. Same root as the XHR latency ramp (/device/time + /otgw/otmonitor climbing 85ms->7000ms). Proven via the new headless-Edge CDP capture: every local .css/.js shows PENDING/never-finished in a 30s window.
fix: Diagnosis only (TASK-821 added the capture tooling that proved it). Real fix is server-side webui responsiveness / HTTP multi-connect drain - TASK-817 territory, not the filesystem. Do NOT chase this as a missing-file/rebuild-LittleFS problem.

error_message: Version banners drifted (alpha.165 in headers vs alpha.170 in version.h); user saw different build numbers
root_cause: Documented bump commit step staged only version.h + data/version.hash; banner updates autoinc-semver.py wrote into ~43 files stayed uncommitted
fix: bump-prerelease.sh runs autoinc-semver.py --update-all --print-updated and git-adds every touched file; CLAUDE.md instructions updated; pending drift committed as sync commit 18a5cc96

error_message: fatal: pathspec 'src/OTGW-firmware/version.h?' did not match any files
root_cause: Windows Python prints \r\n; bash while-read kept trailing \r in filename, git add pathspec failed
fix: strip CR in read loop: f=${f%$'\r'}

error_message: PIC not recognized at all on 2.0.0 esp32-classic (LOLIN S3 Mini on OTGW Classic): 'no pic found', HW_MODE_DEGRADED, zero OT frames over 14.9h, ~893 unanswered PR=A probes
root_cause: OTGWSerial.cpp ESP32 branch guards UART pin choice with #if defined(PIN_PIC_RX)&&defined(PIN_PIC_TX), but boards.h is not in that translation unit's include chain and [env:esp32-classic] passes no -DPIN_PIC_RX/-DPIN_PIC_TX, so the fallback begin(9600,SERIAL_8N1,16,17) compiled in: GPIO16=PIN_LED1 (ledc PWM fights RX), GPIO17=inner-row unconnected. boards.h map itself (RX=44,TX=43) is correct per footprint. Proven via .pio/build/esp32-classic/lib244/OTGWSerial/OTGWSerial.cpp.d containing zero boards.h entries. Also explains why ADR-125 combo detection never converged (combo env equally lacked the flags).
fix: PROPOSED (not yet applied): add -DPIN_PIC_RX=44 -DPIN_PIC_TX=43 to [env:esp32-classic] build_flags in platformio.ini; no vendored-lib edit needed

error_message: python build.py failed: FileExistsError WinError 183 renaming OTGW-firmware-esp32-otgw32.ino.bin to versioned name during esp32-classic artifact collection
root_cause: TARGETS['esp32-classic'] built via {**TARGETS['esp32'], ...} dict spread inherited slug='esp32-otgw32' (slug added by TASK-856 after the spread was written in TASK-854). asset_slug('esp32-classic') returned the otgw32 token, so Classic artifacts copied over the OTGW32 asset names and the version-rename collided. Stale comment claimed filenames derive from the target key.
fix: Explicit slug 'esp32-classic' added to the esp32-classic TARGETS entry + comment corrected (TASK-863)

error_message: TASK-864 combo revival: planned 'if (!isPICEnabled()) initEthernet()' gate not restored; ungated W5500 probe clocks GPIO12 = PIC reset hole on Classic PCB
root_cause: Revival reconstructed detection block from ec55a1fb diff but the Ethernet-gate hunk sat in a different section of setup(); missed during manual revert-adaptation
fix: Restored explicit isPICEnabled() gate around initEthernet(); ADR-127 Decision 6 updated. Caught by adr-generator verification pass (code-vs-claim check)

error_message: pre-commit bump-check would exit 1: TASK-865.4 staged firmware edit to src/libraries/Platform/src/platform_esp32.h but version.h _VERSION_PRERELEASE unchanged (still alpha.178, consumed by TASK-865.2 commit 6bca21ad)
root_cause: platform_esp32.h matches the src/libraries/ TRIGGER in .githooks/pre-commit; the staged version.h diff had no +/- _VERSION_PRERELEASE pair, so PLUS<1 -> hook exit 1. The prior implementer skipped the mandated bump.
fix: Ran bin/bump-prerelease.sh: alpha.178 -> alpha.179, staging version.h + data/version.hash + 42 source-file banners (44 files). Verified hook predicate now sees both -alpha.178 and +alpha.179 lines.

error_message: TASK-865.5 spec said OTFrameMsg.line[10] (9-char OT frame); would truncate PIC lines and break byte-identical output
root_cause: processOT() is fed the FULL PIC line, not just 9-char OT frames: banners, '='-command-echoes, and PS=1 comma-summaries (>256 bytes, MAX_BUFFER_READ=512) route through its else-if branches and produce observable MQTT/WebSocket/OT-log output. A 10-byte queue item truncates them.
fix: OTFrameMsg.line = char[MAX_BUFFER_READ] (512), len = uint16_t (511>255), producer null-terminates line[len] (non-OT branches use strstr/strchr/strcasecmp_P which need a terminator). Hoisted MAX_BUFFER_READ from inside handlePICSerial() to OTGW-Core.h so struct + read loop share one size.

error_message: espMqttClient migration (TASK-865.7): handleMQTT() pumped MQTTclient.loop() only when connected(); with the async client connect() merely queues and loop() is the SOLE driver of the connect handshake, so MQTT would never come up (connected() stays false -> loop() skipped -> handshake never advances).
root_cause: PubSubClient.connect() was synchronous (link up on return), so the 'if (connected()) loop()' guard was correct. espMqttClient::connect() is async; loop() advances connectingTcp->connectingMqtt->CONNACK->onConnect. Gating loop() on connected() deadlocks the handshake.
fix: Pump MQTTclient.loop() UNCONDITIONALLY at top of handleMQTT() (safe no-op when idle). Removed the redundant loop() call inside MQTT_STATE_IS_CONNECTED. Verified against other-projects/EMS-ESP32-dev/lib/espMqttClient/src/MqttClient.cpp.

error_message: TASK-865.8: verify-window discovery counting must tolerate espMqttClient chunked inbound (onMessage index/total) without inflating verifyReceivedCount
root_cause: espMqttClient delivers payloads >=EMC_PAYLOAD_BUFFER_SIZE (32B) in chunks; a naive 'count per onMessage call' would count one ~900B retained config N times, masking genuinely-missing configs and defeating ADR-062.
fix: handleDiscoveryVerifyMessage matches on TOPIC only (ignores length); the onMessage shim's index==0 gate dispatches only the first chunk, which already carries the full topic (variable-header topic parsed before any payload chunk), so each config counts exactly once. Do NOT hoist the verify filter before the index!=0 gate. Also removed the now-defunct RX-buffer resize (setBufferSize/restore no-ops) and max-block preflight; kept VERIFICATION_MIN_HEAP_START.
